name: CausaLab Causal Graph Configuration Dataset
description: Synthetic causal graph configuration suites used to evaluate agentic causal discovery and intervention planning in a crystal-reactor environment. Released alongside the CausaLab paper (NeurIPS 2026 Datasets & Benchmarks) by Dylan Zhang and collaborators at UIUC, Tsinghua, CMU, University of Chicago, and Adobe.

distribution.name: 4nodes.jsonl
distribution.description: Main 4-node graph suite; baseline for several ablations and all 4-node scaling runs

distribution.name: 6nodes.jsonl
distribution.description: Main 6-node graph suite; all 6-node scaling runs

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_n1.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise count sweep with one affected observable node

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_n2.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise count sweep with two affected observable nodes

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_n3.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise count sweep with three affected observable nodes

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_vrange_pm0p5.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise range sweep with perturbation values +/-0.5

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_vrange_pm5.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise range sweep with perturbation values +/-5

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_vrange_pm50.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-noise range sweep with perturbation values +/-50

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_freqnode_n1.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-freqnode sweep with one affected node including resonance frequency

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_freqnode_n2.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-freqnode sweep with two affected nodes including resonance frequency

distribution.name: 4nodes_hidden_freqnode_n3.jsonl
distribution.description: Hidden-freqnode sweep with three affected nodes including resonance frequency

recordSet.name: causal_graph_config_records
recordSet.description: Each JSONL line is one synthetic causal graph configuration used as an evaluation task.

field.name: graph_id
field.description: Unique graph/task identifier within a suite.

field.name: description
field.description: Natural-language description of the causal graph family and edge count.

field.name: nodes
field.description: Mapping from variable names to controllability, observability, display metadata, base values, and structural equations.

field.name: edges
field.description: Directed causal edges as from/to pairs.

field.name: params
field.description: Numerical parameters used by the structural equations.

field.name: budget
field.description: Default intervention budget for the graph suite.

field.name: hidden_variable
field.description: Optional hidden perturbation configuration for hidden-noise suites.

field.name: bootstrap_past_data
field.description: Optional static intervention traces for Golden suites.

field.name: frequency_can_be_parent
field.description: Optional flag for FreqParent suites where resonance frequency may have outgoing edges.

field.name: quadratic_metadata
field.description: Optional metadata for hard-quadratic mechanism suites.

rai:dataUseCases: Intended for evaluating causal graph discovery, intervention planning, and target-frequency prediction in synthetic agentic environments.
rai:dataLimitations: The dataset consists of synthetic graph configurations and does not represent real physical systems. Performance on these tasks should not be interpreted as evidence of real-world scientific discovery ability.
rai:dataBiases: Graph topologies, variable names, coefficients, hidden perturbations, and observation/intervention budgets reflect controlled generator choices. Models may exploit these synthetic regularities.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: The dataset contains no personal or sensitive information.
rai:dataSocialImpact: The dataset supports controlled evaluation of causal reasoning behavior. Misuse risk is limited by its synthetic nature, but results should be reported as benchmark behavior rather than real-world causal competence.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Synthetic causal graph generators and follow-up suite generation scripts described in the CausaLab paper. Authored by Dylan Zhang and collaborators (UIUC, Tsinghua, CMU, University of Chicago, Adobe).
